text.title: Instagram Growth Metrics and Common Mistakes
text.summary: KPI benchmarks by growth stage, engagement rate targets, and the most common Instagram growth failure modes.

rule.statement: Track Reel reach % (not just views), engagement rate, Story completion rate, and weekly follower growth rate as primary KPIs.
rule.rationale: Raw view counts are misleading for small accounts. Reach % relative to follower count indicates whether Explore distribution is working.

list.item: Reel reach: 10% of followers = average, 30% = good, 100%+ = viral

antipattern.description: Only posting photos instead of Reels.
antipattern.reason: Photos reach only existing followers. Reels reach Explore audiences. Without Reels, organic growth stalls.

antipattern.description: Inconsistent posting cadence — missing days or weeks.
antipattern.reason: Instagram's algorithm penalizes accounts with erratic posting patterns by reducing distribution. Batch-film content to maintain consistency.
Raw, authentic content currently outperforms polished production on Instagram. Audience trust comes from authenticity, not production value.

antipattern.description: No face on camera for personal brand accounts.
antipattern.reason: Accounts showing a real person consistently achieve higher engagement than faceless brand accounts in the same niche.
DM conversations are the highest-conversion touchpoint on Instagram. Delayed responses lose leads that were ready to buy.

rule.statement: Agent-generated Reel scripts must be reviewed for hook quality before filming. The first 1-3 seconds determine watch time; the agent cannot judge if a hook is compelling in delivery.
rule.rationale: Script quality and on-camera delivery are separate skills. Agents produce structure; humans provide authenticity in delivery.

rule.statement: Instagram Graph API provides page-level insights only. Personal account insights require manual export from the Instagram app before agent analysis.
rule.rationale: The API does not expose individual post performance for personal accounts. Agents cannot pull this data autonomously.
